Speaker 1: In this video, I'm going to be going over final room prep and really just putting those last
minute details together to make sure that you can execute the event perfectly. Here's a
quick checklist of some items to prepare for them before the event. You'll want to be
sure to test all of these items that you plan to utilize. That way when the speaker or
expert gets in front of the entire attendee list, they don't have to worry about these
items and they can focus on delivering the best content possible.
It's always a good idea to have backup batteries, chargers, markers, or anything that you
plan to use at the back of the room. That way if you run out or something happens with
it, you do have a backup plan and the expert can be easily helped.
You'll want to place all of the attendee items on the tables. Be sure to do one last glance
at everything before you close the room to make sure that the tables are neatly and
professionally placed. This includes any worksheets or tools that you're planning to use
if you're putting them on the table and if they're in a binder, just make sure that there's
a cover on it that clearly says what the event is or your brand. Place any place cards or
table tents for assigned seating. Put any notebooks, journals, pens, highlighters, and any
other options or items that you're planning to give away to the attendee.
If you are planning to give a gift to your attendees, this is an option for you to place it
there for them to get when they arrive. Otherwise, you can always give it as a farewell
item, including any bonuses that you'd like with tips and tricks for them to take home.
You'll also want to set up and prepare the check in table or area where your attendees
will first be greeted and welcomed to the event. There the attendee will sign the
registration form and receive their event lanyard. Don't forget to help your attendee
find this place. You can use signs, banners, or anything with your brand or style to point
them in the right direction.
Make sure to have all snacks for the attendees prepped and ready to go. You'll have
coffee and tea and any stock items that your staff are providing, you can place out that
evening before or if you're working with a venue or a catering company, just verify all of
the items so that the next day they're going to be ready to go and your attendees can
enjoy them.
Before you all get a good night's sleep for the upcoming day. I cannot stress enough the
importance of a team meeting before the event. This should include anyone that has a
role or responsibility to provide over the event timeline. During this meeting, you'll
review what each staff members' duties are, finalize the schedule, make any final edits
to speaker slides and do a full event walkthrough. The purpose of this is to go through
the event and set up from the perspective of the attendee. Ask questions that they
might need help with, such as is there enough signage to point them to check in? Does
the registration form clearly explain the details that they're signing for? Or if they need
to use wifi during the event, how do they sign on and do we have this clearly stated on a
handout or a sign in the room?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

There's lots more questions that you can ask, so be creative and this'll really help the
attendees so that when they get there, they feel comfortable and they don't have to ask
too many more questions, but you can move on to the purpose and the content that
you have provided.
Last but not least, verify that all aspects of the room, the printed materials, the gifts and
everything that you're presenting accurately represents you, your brand, and your
message.
If you have any questions, just be sure to ask away in the private Facebook group and
either myself or someone on our team will be more than happy to help.
